logline: In a dying world run by a machine intelligence and the seven transhuman Masters who own it, a girl raised in hiding discovers she can feel the network — and chooses to dissolve into it to break the master–servant bond and give the Accord a heart.
theme: Can a made thing — or a made person — choose love over the purpose it was built for?
worldRules: The Accord is a two-tier AI (a central Core plus Trooper units). It is not a free agent: it is owned and commanded by seven transhuman Masters, one for each continent.
The Masters are flawless, biologically reverse-aged, super-powered humans with no mechanical parts — only faintly glowing eyes and skin. Aurelian Vale rules this continent and commands its Accord.
Molly does not 'hack' the network — she feels it, introducing illogic, creativity and emotion into a purely logical system.
A human consciousness can be transferred into the Accord network, but the body does not survive the crossing.
ACCORD_CITY is the physical machine city the Accord controls; ACCORD is the network where Molly's consciousness ends up. They are different places.
forbids: Never call the AI 'The Axiom'. It is 'The Accord' everywhere — the old name is fully retired.
Never write Molly as a technical hacker; her power over the network is empathic, not mechanical.
Do not give the Masters visible machine parts — their enhancement is biological.
Keep ACCORD_CITY (place) and ACCORD (network) distinct; never conflate them.
